text: {"analysis_summary": "Reviewed the visible RTL/source evidence under the provided scope, focusing on JTAG/debug access paths and permission/authorization controls. The design exposes external JTAG pads at the top level, routes them through safe_domain/jtag_tap_top into SoC debug selection signals, and includes an Advanced Debug Interface capable of acting as an AXI master and CPU debug controller. The visible code shows debug module selection and bus transactions are driven solely by JTAG TAP instructions/shifted commands, with no visible authentication, lock, privilege check, lifecycle fuse, production-disable gate, or permission policy. A suspicious commented PASSWORD value exists, but it is not implemented as logic and explicitly says “but not really.” Based on the visible evidence, this is a permission-related security vulnerability: an entity with JTAG access can select debug modules and generate AXI reads/writes and CPU control actions without authorization enforcement in the RTL shown.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "PERM-JTAG-DEBUG-UNAUTH-001", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "Unauthenticated JTAG/advanced debug interface can select debug modules and initiate AXI/CPU debug operations without visible permission enforcement.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization / improper permission control on hardware debug interface", "affected_locations": [{"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 81, "line_end": 81, "module": "pulpissimo", "signal_or_register": "pad_jtag_tck / pad_jtag_tdi / pad_jtag_tdo / pad_jtag_tms / pad_jtag_trst"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 566, "line_end": 566, "module": "pulpissimo", "signal_or_register": "s_jtag_tck to safe_domain .jtag_tck_i"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/safe_domain.sv", "line_start": 453, "line_end": 474, "module": "safe_domain", "signal_or_register": "jtag_tap_top_i / axireg_sel_o"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 57, "line_end": 93, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "signal_or_register": "tap_top_i, axireg_sel_o, dbg_axi_scan_in_o, dbg_axi_scan_out_i"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 30, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "test_mode_i, s_debug_select, dbg_module_i.debug_select_i"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 218, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "debug_select_i, module_id_reg, module_selects[0]"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 289, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "debug_select_i, module_selects[1]"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 335, "line_end": 540, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "signal_or_register": "addr_reg, axi_master_ar_addr, axi_master_aw_addr, axi_master_w_data, axi_master_aw_valid, axi_master_ar_valid, axi_master_w_valid"}], "evidence": [{"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 81, "line_end": 81, "module": "pulpissimo", "object": "pad_jtag_tck / pad_jtag_tdi / pad_jtag_tdo / pad_jtag_tms / pad_jtag_trst", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "Top-level module exposes JTAG pads as external inout wires, including TCK/TDI/TDO/TMS/TRST.", "supports_claim": "External JTAG access is part of the top-level hardware interface."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 566, "line_end": 566, "module": "pulpissimo", "object": ".jtag_tck_i ( s_jtag_tck )", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "Top-level connects JTAG clock signal into a downstream jtag_tck_i port.", "supports_claim": "External JTAG is routed into internal debug/TAP logic."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/safe_domain.sv", "line_start": 453, "line_end": 474, "module": "safe_domain", "object": "jtag_tap_top_i / axireg_sel_o", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "safe_domain instantiates jtag_tap_top and connects axireg_sel_o.", "supports_claim": "JTAG TAP logic generates a debug/AXI register select signal used by the SoC debug path."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 57, "line_end": 93, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "object": "tap_top_i and dbg_axi_scan path", "evidence_type": "source_excerpt", "description": "jtag_tap_top forwards JTAG state and select signals from tap_top, including axireg_sel_o, and connects scan path to dbg_axi_scan_in_o/dbg_axi_scan_out_i.", "supports_claim": "JTAG commands can select and drive the AXI debug scan path; no authorization signal is visible in this wrapper."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 130, "line_end": 134, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "object": "soc_jtag_reg_o / sel_fll_clk_o", "evidence_type": "source_excerpt", "description": "jtag_tap_top assigns SoC JTAG outputs directly from the TAP/conf register results, including soc_jtag_reg_o and sel_fll_clk_o.", "supports_claim": "JTAG-controlled configuration values affect SoC-visible control outputs without visible permission checks."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 30, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "JTAG pads, adbg_tap_top, adbg_top.debug_select_i", "evidence_type": "source_excerpt", "description": "adv_dbg_if has raw JTAG pad inputs/outputs and a test_mode_i input, and instantiates adbg_tap_top and adbg_top, passing s_debug_select to debug_select_i.", "supports_claim": "Debug module selection is derived from TAP state and wired directly into the debug module; no password/authentication gate is visible."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 12, "line_end": 12, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "commented PASSWORD", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "A commented PASSWORD line exists but is explicitly non-functional: “//PASSWORD = 0x1c10_0001; //but not really”.", "supports_claim": "Visible source contains no implemented password check; the only password-like artifact is a comment."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 170, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "module_id_reg <= module_id_in", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "adbg_top latches module_id_reg from JTAG-shifted module_id_in when debug_select_i, select_cmd, update_dr_i and !select_inhibit are true.", "supports_claim": "Module selection is controlled by JTAG debug_select and shifted command state rather than an authorization check."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 176, "line_end": 289, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "module_selects / module_select_i", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "adbg_top selects individual modules with module_selects = 2'b01 and module_selects = 2'b10, then passes module_selects[0] and module_selects[1] to submodules.", "supports_claim": "JTAG-selected module IDs enable debug submodules directly."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 66, "line_end": 75, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "cpu_stall_o / cpu_we_o / cpu_rst_o", "evidence_type": "source_excerpt", "description": "adbg_top exposes CPU debug control outputs such as cpu_stall_o, cpu_we_o, and cpu_rst_o in its port list.", "supports_claim": "The debug interface can affect CPU execution/control if selected."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 335, "line_end": 383, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "addr_reg / axi_master_ar_addr / axi_master_aw_addr / axi_master_w_data", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "adbg_axi_biu latches addr_reg from addr_i and assigns AXI read/write addresses and write data from debug-controlled registers.", "supports_claim": "The debug interface can issue AXI transactions to arbitrary debug-provided addresses/data."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 509, "line_end": 540, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "axi_master_aw_valid / axi_master_ar_valid / axi_master_w_valid", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "adbg_axi_biu drives AXI transaction valid signals high in its state machine: axi_master_aw_valid, axi_master_ar_valid, and axi_master_w_valid.", "supports_claim": "The debug BIU can actively initiate AXI reads and writes."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 396, "line_end": 396, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "axi_master_aw_prot", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "adbg_axi_biu assigns AXI protection field to zero rather than deriving it from a security/privilege permission policy.", "supports_claim": "No visible AXI privilege/security authorization metadata enforcement is present in this BIU."}], "reasoning_summary": "The visible design contains an externally accessible JTAG/debug path. The TAP selects debug logic and feeds debug_select_i/module_select_i without any visible authentication or permission predicate. Once selected, adbg_top exposes CPU control signals and instantiates an AXI debug module; adbg_axi_biu latches debug-provided address/data and drives AXI read/write valid signals. The only password-related evidence is a comment, not RTL. Therefore the RTL shown permits debug-originated bus and CPU control operations based on JTAG commands alone, which is a permission bypass if this interface is present in a deployed or security-sensitive configuration.", "security_impact": "An attacker with physical or logical access to the JTAG pins may be able to select the debug module and issue arbitrary AXI read/write transactions, inspect or modify memory-mapped state, alter configuration registers, stall/reset/control CPUs, bypass software privilege boundaries, extract secrets, patch code/data, or gain persistent control. If this design is used in a production SoC without external mitigation, the impact is high: complete compromise of confidentiality, integrity, and execution control.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The analysis is limited to visible files and tool outputs. Some files were truncated, and no simulation/formal analysis was performed. It is possible that an external wrapper, lifecycle controller, fuse, board strapping, or access-control block outside the visible evidence disables JTAG or gates these signals in production. Within the inspected source evidence, no such authorization mechanism was visible.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Add a hardware-enforced debug authorization gate, e.g. lifecycle/debug_disable fuse, secure boot authenticated unlock, or irreversible production lock, and require it before asserting debug_select_i/module_select_i or AXI/CPU debug strobes.", "Ensure the JTAG TAP defaults to disabled in production and cannot be enabled by software alone after secure boot policy locks are set.", "Gate adbg_axi_biu transaction strobes and CPU debug outputs with an explicit authenticated_debug_en signal.", "Constrain debug AXI access through an access-control/filter block if partial debug access is required.", "Review pad mux and board-level accessibility to ensure JTAG pads are not exposed or are disabled in production.", "Verify with security tests/formal properties that unauthenticated JTAG sequences cannot assert axi_master_*_valid, cpu_stall_o, cpu_we_o, or cpu_rst_o."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "The report is based only on source evidence visible through the permitted tools before the tool-step limit was reached. The broader integration, synthesis defines, production configuration, and any off-scope security controller were not available. Therefore the finding is confirmed for the visible RTL behavior but deployment impact depends on whether external/off-scope mitigations disable or gate this debug path."}
